sample_size.source_quote: Overall 138 patients met the inclusion criteria, including 74 survivors and 64 non-survivors.

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Lactate level, SOFA score, PCT level, APACHE II score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Binary logistic regression showed that the presepsin level (Odds ratio (OR) =1.221, 95% confidence interval (CI): 1.024–1.455, P=0.026)... The best prognostic cutoff point for presepsin was 2,623 pg/mL, at which level the sensitivity and specificity were 62.71% (95% CI: 49.15% to 74.96%) and 72.6% (95% CI: 60.91% to 82.39%), respectively.

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Presepsin level, SOFA score, PCT level, APACHE II score
Presepsin level, lactate level, PCT level, APACHE II score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: SOFA score (OR =1.404, 95% CI: 1.209–1.632, P=0.000) were risk factors for patients' in-hospital mortality from sepsis... The SOFA score had the best performance with the highest AUC value (AUC =0.793, P=0.000).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Combination of SOFA score and presepsin level
mortality_predictors.source_quote: the AUC value of a combination of the SOFA score and the presepsin level was significantly higher than that of the SOFA score alone in predicting patients' in-hospital mortality (AUC: 0.817 vs 0.793, P=0.041).

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Presepsin level, lactate level, SOFA score, APACHE II score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: there was no association between patients' in-hospital mortality and their PCT levels (OR =0.995, 95% CI: 0.957–1.089, P=0.527)

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Presepsin level, lactate level, SOFA score, PCT level
